content: Just as the educational definition of reflection frames it as a deliberate metacognitive practice for human learners to consolidate new knowledge from experience, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this pedagogical framing to move beyond niche AI debugging toward a structured skill-building routine that mimics how human students iteratively improve. This means we can design LLM self-reflection schedules aligned with the spaced reflection frameworks used in high school literacy curricula to boost long-term task performance, rather than only using reflection to correct single wrong outputs.
